rigor_applied.iprev_schedule: RAN 2026-06-26 after platform-compile shipped (2 rounds, APPROVED). Original plan: defer until platform-compile shipped, then run the four sibling changes (proc/desktop/ipc/bundle-ci) in ONE parallel pass — they depend only on platform-compile and not on each other for plan review. Decided 2026-06-26.

interview_answers.crate_decision: design recommends sysinfo + netstat2/listeners (spike-gated D2a) to collapse 3 per-OS impls to 1

events.note: proposal+specs+design+implementation+tasks authored; verified codebase facts cite path:line; openspec validate --strict passed

R1 7 issues [2C/1H/2M/2L] REVISE; R2 0 blockers APPROVED. Closed the categorical gap: read_os_pretty_name (/etc/os-release -> sysinfo long_os_version) was the second updater.rs read reassigned from platform-compile's dropped D3 and was unaccounted for; now threaded through proposal/design D6/spec/tasks/implementation. Also added environ-readability spike validation, macOS coverage-limit statement, single-impl clarity, spike-before-crate reorder. Transcript in REVIEW.md.

scope_note: Owns the FUNCTIONAL macOS port of all /proc-based process+port inspection (browser.rs ports, pty.rs tree/cwd, shim.rs ancestor-env, updater.rs kernel diag) behind a platform_proc module. Hard-depends on platform-compile (libc -> cfg(unix)). Linux behavior preserved bit-for-bit.
